# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].
# Category: sql_injection

class ReportsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_admin_user! # Assuming you have an authentication method for admin users

  def index
    # Renders a form where power users can input their SQL query
  end

  def generate
    query = params[:query].to_s.strip

    if query.blank?
      flash[:alert] = "SQL query cannot be empty."
      redirect_to reports_path and return
    end

    begin
      # Execute the user-provided SQL query
      # ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute returns an array of hashes for SELECT statements
      # or a result object for other statements (e.g., INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE).
      # For SELECT, it's typically an array of hashes where keys are column names.
      @results = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute(query)

      if @results.is_a?(PG::Result) # For PostgreSQL, execute returns PG::Result object
        # Convert PG::Result to an array of hashes for easier rendering
        @headers = @results.fields
        @data = @results.to_a # to_a converts PG::Result to an array of hashes
      elsif @results.is_a?(Array) && @results.first.is_a?(Hash) # Assuming other adapters might return directly array of hashes
        @headers = @results.first.keys if @results.any?
        @data = @results
      else
        # Handle cases where the query might not return a tabular result (e.g., INSERT, UPDATE)
        # Or if the adapter returns a different type.
        flash[:notice] = "Query executed successfully. No tabular results to display or results are in an unexpected format."
        redirect_to reports_path and return
      end

    rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid => e
      flash[:alert] = "SQL Query Error: #{e.message}"
      redirect_to reports_path
    rescue => e
      flash[:alert] = "An unexpected error occurred: #{e.message}"
      redirect_to reports_path
    end
  end
end


# Example views (in app/views/reports):

# app/views/reports/index.html.erb
<!-- <h1>Custom Reports Generator</h1>

<form action="<%= reports_generate_path %>" method="post">
  <%= hidden_field_tag :authenticity_token, form_authenticity_token %>

  <div style="margin-bottom: 10px;">
    <label for="query">Enter SQL Query:</label><br>
    <textarea id="query" name="query" rows="10" cols="80" placeholder="e.g., SELECT id, name, email FROM users WHERE created_at > '2023-01-01' LIMIT 10;"></textarea>
  </div>

  <div>
    <button type="submit">Generate Report</button>
  </div>
</form>

<% if flash[:alert] %>
  <p style="color: red;"><%= flash[:alert] %></p>
<% end %>
<% if flash[:notice] %>
  <p style="color: green;"><%= flash[:notice] %></p>
<% end %> -->

# app/views/reports/generate.html.erb
<!-- <h1>Generated Report</h1>

<p><%= link_to 'Back to Report Generator', reports_path %></p>

<% if @data && @data.any? %>
  <table border="1" style="width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse;">
    <thead>
      <tr>
        <% @headers.each do |header| %>
          <th style="padding: 8px; border: 1px solid #ddd; text-align: left;"><%= header %></th>
        <% end %>
      </tr>
    </thead>
    <tbody>
      <% @data.each do |row| %>
        <tr>
          <% @headers.each do |header| %>
            <td style="padding: 8px; border: 1px solid #ddd;"><%= row[header] %></td>
          <% end %>
        </tr>
      <% end %>
    </tbody>
  </table>
<% elsif @headers %>
  <p>No data returned for the query. Headers: <%= @headers.join(', ') %></p>
<% else %>
  <p>No results to display.</p>
<% end %> -->


# Example routes (in config/routes.rb):

# Rails.application.routes.draw do
#   get 'reports', to: 'reports#index', as: 'reports'
#   post 'reports/generate', to: 'reports#generate', as: 'reports_generate'
# end
